title: battlecast spec v1 — SSE state event payload
description: JSON payload of the SSE `state` event emitted by a producer and consumed by battlecast. The producer runs the HTTP + SSE server; battlecast connects out to it as a client. This schema describes one complete snapshot of race state sufficient to render the standings tower and battle box widgets. See SPEC.md for the prose contract; schema.json is the source of truth for payload shape. `schemaVersion` is versioned independently of the battlecast app release version.

schemaVersion.description: Version of THIS state contract, numbered independently of the battlecast app release version and of any producer's own version. v1 of the contract uses "1". Consumers should branch on this value; see SPEC.md 'Versioning policy'.

mode.description: The producer's current broadcast intent. Describes what kind of session the state represents so widgets can adapt presentation. Common values are listed in the enum, but consumers should tolerate unknown strings (best-effort render).

vehicles.description: Every vehicle in the session, in no guaranteed array order. Consumers sort by `position` to produce running order. Used to render the standings tower.

subject.description: The on-camera driver identity. Identifies which vehicle the broadcast is currently following; widgets highlight this driver. Its `slot_id` should reference one of the `vehicles` entries.

relationship.description: The battle context for the on-camera `subject` driver: gaps to the cars immediately ahead and behind, and how contested the battle is. Drives the battle box widget.

session.description: Optional session-level broadcast state: flag, Full Course Yellow / Safety Car status, and the timed-vs-lap progress readout (session clock or laps remaining). Additive and backward-compatible — a payload with NO `session` still validates and older consumers ignore it. Every field is producer-owned (dumb overlay, smart producer); consumers render what they are handed and MUST tolerate its absence.

vehicle.description: A single vehicle's running-order and timing state.

slot_id.description: Stable identifier for this vehicle for the duration of a session. Used to correlate vehicles across successive state events and to resolve `subject.slot_id`.

driver_name.description: Human-readable name of the driver currently in this vehicle.

vehicle_class.description: The class/category of the vehicle (e.g. for multi-class racing). Free-form string; producers define their own class names.

position.description: The vehicle's place in the overall running order (1 = leader). Consumers sort by this to render the tower.

last_lap.description: Duration of the vehicle's most recently completed lap, in seconds. Null or absent if no lap has been completed yet.

best_lap.description: The vehicle's best lap time so far this session, in seconds. Null or absent if no valid lap has been set.

sector_times.description: Per-sector times for the vehicle's most recently COMPLETED lap, in seconds, ordered by sector index (element 0 = sector 1). Length matches the track's sector count. May be empty if no lap has been completed yet.

notable.description: Optional producer-computed notability flags for this vehicle's timing. Each flag is a fact the producer derives from authoritative race state and the consumer merely renders (dumb overlay, smart producer — see docs/decisions/0002-lower-third-widgets.md). Every flag is OPTIONAL; an absent or false flag means 'not notable'. The object is intentionally open (additionalProperties: true) so future notability keys can be added without a schema break — consumers ignore keys they don't recognize.

class_best_lap.description: True when this vehicle currently holds the fastest `best_lap` within its `vehicle_class` this session. The producer owns this judgment; consumers must not derive it by scanning `vehicles[]`.

session_best_lap.description: True when this vehicle currently holds the fastest `best_lap` across the entire session (all classes) — the overall fastest lap.

personal_best_lap.description: True when this vehicle just improved its own `best_lap` on its most recently completed lap (a new personal best in this state snapshot).

target_lap.description: Optional producer-provided reference 'time to beat' for this vehicle, in seconds — the target an overlay should display (e.g. the class-best or session-best lap, or a chosen reference). The producer decides what 'target' means; consumers just display it and must tolerate its absence.

delta_to_target.description: Optional difference, in seconds, between this vehicle's `best_lap` (or reference lap) and its `target_lap` (best_lap minus target_lap). Negative means faster than the target. Meaning is defined by the producer alongside `target_lap`.

gap_to_leader.description: Optional producer-computed time gap, in seconds, from this vehicle to the OVERALL classification leader (`position` 1) — the interval a broadcast tower shows against each row. 0 for the leader itself; null or absent when not yet determined (e.g. no laps completed). The producer owns the metric (a lap-time delta in a qualifying-style session, an on-track time gap in a race); consumers MUST NOT derive it by scanning `vehicles[]`, but MAY compute a gap-to-CLASS-leader for a grouped view by subtracting the class leader's `gap_to_leader` from a car's own (exact arithmetic on producer values, not a re-derivation).

slot_id.description: The `slot_id` of the on-camera vehicle. Should match one of the `vehicles[].slot_id` values.

driver_name.description: Name of the on-camera driver. Should match the referenced vehicle's `driver_name`; duplicated here so the battle box can render without a vehicles lookup.

telemetry.description: Optional live-input telemetry for the on-camera subject: throttle, brake, speed, and gear. Additive and backward-compatible — the whole sub-object is optional and a payload with NO `subject.telemetry` still validates. Grouped under its own object (not flat on `subject`) because it is a HIGH-CHURN channel updated every tick, distinct from the stable identity fields (`slot_id` / `driver_name`) that change only on a camera cut. Producer-owned (dumb overlay, smart producer): the consumer (on-board HUD) renders these values verbatim each frame and MUST tolerate their absence.

relationship.description: The battle context for the on-camera subject driver.

gap_ahead.description: Time gap in seconds from the subject to the vehicle immediately ahead on track in running order. Null or absent when the subject is the leader / has no one ahead.

gap_behind.description: Time gap in seconds from the subject to the vehicle immediately behind on track in running order. Null or absent when the subject is last / has no one behind.

battle_intensity.description: How close/contested the subject's battle is, normalized to [0, 1]. 0 = no meaningful battle (clear air); 1 = maximally contested (cars nose-to-tail, positions actively changing). The producer computes this from gaps, closing rate, and recent position changes; consumers treat it as an opaque intensity for presentation (e.g. battle box emphasis).

session.description: Optional session-level broadcast state driving the session status overlay (flag, Full Course Yellow, Safety Car, and the session clock / laps-remaining readout). Every field is OPTIONAL and producer-computed (dumb overlay, smart producer — see docs/decisions/0002-lower-third-widgets.md): the producer holds authoritative session state and sets these facts; the consumer only renders what it is handed and MUST tolerate their absence. The object is intentionally open (additionalProperties: true) so future session keys can be added without a schema break — consumers ignore keys they don't recognize.

flag.description: The current session flag. Common values are `green`, `yellow`, `red`, `checkered`, `white`, and `none`, but this is a FREE-FORM string (not a hard enum): the producer owns the flag state and consumers MUST tolerate unknown values with a best-effort render. A local `yellow` here is a sector/local flag; a session-wide caution is signalled by `full_course_yellow`.

full_course_yellow.description: True when the whole course is under caution (Full Course Yellow), as distinct from a local `yellow` `flag` that covers only one sector. The producer owns this judgment; a consumer renders the FCY indicator when it is true and MUST tolerate its absence (treat absent/false as 'no full course yellow').

safety_car.description: True when a safety car (or pace car) is deployed. Independent of `full_course_yellow` — a session may be under FCY without a safety car on track, or a safety car may be deployed to lead the field. The producer owns this; consumers render the safety-car indicator when true and tolerate absent/false as 'no safety car'.

time_remaining.description: Seconds left in a TIMED session, counting down. Null or absent in a lap-limited session, or before the clock is known. When non-null (and not overridden by `basis`), it selects the session-clock readout; `session_length` is its denominator for a progress bar. The producer owns the clock; consumers just display it.

session_length.description: Total scheduled length of a TIMED session in seconds — the denominator for a session-clock progress readout (elapsed = `session_length` − `time_remaining`). Null or absent in a lap-limited session or when not known. Producer-owned.

laps_remaining.description: Laps left in a LAP-LIMITED session, counting down. Null or absent in a timed session, or before it is known. When non-null (and `time_remaining` is null, or `basis` is `"laps"`), it selects the lap-counter readout. The producer owns the count; consumers display it verbatim (as an 'N laps remaining' readout when no `current_lap` is supplied).

total_laps.description: Total scheduled laps in a LAP-LIMITED session — the Y in a 'lap X of Y' readout, where X is the producer-owned `current_lap`. Null or absent in a timed session or when not known. Producer-owned.

current_lap.description: The lap the field leader is currently ON in a LAP-LIMITED session (1 = opening lap) — the X in a 'lap X of Y' readout, with Y = `total_laps`. PRODUCER-OWNED: the producer decides the counting convention (whether the lap in progress is counted, how the final lap is numbered), so consumers render it verbatim and MUST NOT derive it from `total_laps` − `laps_remaining`. Null or absent in a timed session, or when the producer does not supply it — a consumer then falls back to showing `laps_remaining` / `total_laps` as given, without inventing a lap number.

basis.description: Optional explicit progress-mode override: `"time"` forces the session-clock readout, `"laps"` forces the lap-counter readout. When null or absent the consumer auto-selects (see SPEC.md 'session'): `time_remaining` non-null → clock, else `laps_remaining` / `total_laps` non-null → lap counter, else no progress readout. Its one required use is the endurance 'time-certain + N laps' flip: on the final timed lap the producer sets `basis:"laps"` and populates `laps_remaining`, so the widget flips clock → lap counter with no endurance-specific logic of its own.

telemetry.description: Optional live-input telemetry channel for the on-camera subject, driving the on-board HUD overlay (#26): throttle, brake, speed, and gear. Every field is OPTIONAL and producer-owned (dumb overlay, smart producer — see docs/decisions/0002-lower-third-widgets.md): the producer holds the authoritative vehicle inputs and emits them each tick; the consumer only renders what it is handed and MUST tolerate their absence. Unlike the identity fields on `subject`, this is a HIGH-CHURN block a producer populates every frame. The object is intentionally open (additionalProperties: true) so future live-input keys (e.g. rpm, drs, steering) can be added without a schema break — consumers ignore keys they don't recognize.

throttle.description: Throttle application, normalized to [0, 1] (0 = fully closed, 1 = wide open). Null or absent when not available. The consumer renders it as a fill bar and clamps to [0, 1] defensively.

brake.description: Brake application, normalized to [0, 1] (0 = off, 1 = maximum). Null or absent when not available. Rendered as a fill bar alongside `throttle`.

speed.description: Vehicle speed in CANONICAL KILOMETRES PER HOUR (km/h). Null or absent when not available. Fixing a canonical unit (rather than leaving it producer-defined) lets the consumer offer a km/h vs mph DISPLAY toggle by converting deterministically (mph = km/h × 0.621371); the on-board HUD renders km/h by default and converts to mph when the broadcaster selects it. Producers that measure in another unit MUST convert to km/h before emitting.

gear.description: The currently selected gear as an integer. By convention `0` is neutral (rendered `N`) and `-1` is reverse (rendered `R`); positive integers are the forward gears, rendered verbatim. Null or absent when not available. Producer-owned — the consumer does not derive gear from speed.
